title: Funding | NUS Cultural Activities Club (CAC)
content: this website is under development. check in again on 1 august 2026. nus cac home venue funding publicity resources student groups equipment loaning about nus cac home venue funding publicity resources student groups equipment loaning about funds & grants the heartbeat of student creativity. general finance resources nus cac can decide what to add here, any links to link. nus cac can decide what to add here, any links to link. nus cac can decide what to add here, any links to link. nus cac can decide what to add here, any links to link. downloadable infographic to be inserted. nussu council funding this funding scheme is geared towards students’ interests in spearheading their own initiatives, and in doing so, it encourages a spirit of community vibrancy and dynamism. apply now usparks grant the u-sparks funding grant aims to a promote sporting lifestyle, arts appreciation and cultural understanding among the nus community and external communities. apply now nus performing and visual arts scholarship (mid-term) the nus performing & visual arts scholarship is awarded to highly-talented individuals who possess outstanding academic, performing and/or visual arts participation and representation records. apply now cac creator grant our experienced architects provide personalized design solutions that turn your dreams into reality. apply now nussu space credits funding this funding scheme bolsters nus student groups organizing student-led activities and enhance student life on campus. this initiative is designed ito alleviate the operational expenses of student groups and encourage the optimal use of nus facilities. apply now apply now national arts council (nac) funding the nac provides a sustainable funding infrastructure through grants and arts housing support for the arts community to achieve a vision of singapore as home to diverse and distinctive arts that inspire our people, connect our communities and position singapore globally. nussu adobe creative cloud licensing scheme the nus students' union (nussu) offers a limited monthly adobe creative cloud licensing scheme to all general undergraduates, allowing access to desktop applications like photoshop, illustrator, and premiere pro. apply now apply for grants reach us info.nuscac@gmail.com where we are nus students' cultural activities club yusof ishak house 31 lower kent ridge road nus cultural activities club about discover nus arts on campus © 2026 national university of singapore, last updated 16 july 2026

title: Publicity Resources | NUS Cultural Activities Club (CAC)
content: this website is under development. check in again on 1 august 2026. nus cac home venue funding publicity resources student groups equipment loaning about nus cac home venue funding publicity resources student groups equipment loaning about publicity resources make your voices heard. step 1: digital & social media channels osay! e-newsletter best for: reaching the broader student community to promote events, workshops, recruitments, and contests both on and off campus. artwork requirements: image must be exactly 600 × 400 pixels (px) . how to submit: fill out the osay! publicity form . for queries, email osay@nus.edu.sg . nussu platforms best for: student-led activities and events on campus, designed for nussu bodies, registered student organisations, and nus offices. channels included: telegram channel, electronic digital mailer (edm), instagram story repost, and general noticeboard. how to submit: fill out the nussu publicity request form (submit a separate form if you want to use multiple platforms). for general noticeboards, email nussu.communicationssec@u.nus.edu . apply now deadline: 3 days before your publication date nus arts & cfa channels nus arts on campus telegram group: best for arts events on campus or open calls for participants. you can post across 3 distinct topics so students can find what they need (cfa announcements, call for collaborations, upcoming productions, general faq). nus cfa instagram: features instagram stories and posts for relevant arts promotion. digital noticeboard (com3): available for digital display. apply now deadline: 2 weeks prior to your intended promotion date. osa dabble & discover best for: beginner-friendly activities led by student instructors. note: check with ebelle to see if regular students can write in to osa/elissa to request publicity on this platform. (tbc) join now! step 2: setting up official portal events (nusync) if you want to invite all students to join your student-body-led activities (on or off campus), you should host them on: nusync event creation guide important: after saving, your group may need to submit additional information to campus administrators and await final approval before the event goes live. for technical help, view the: step 3: physical campus media workflow the physical poster approval checklist if you want to promote your event via physical posters on campus noticeboards, you must follow these steps in order: [ ] step 1: get digital approval first – ensure your event is officially created and approved on nusync, and your poster designs are approved by your staff advisor. [ ] step 2: check location-specific permissions – depending on where you want to put posters, make sure you have permissions from the respective venue owners: open notice boards: osa bus stops: nussu utown: utown admin halls, residences & faculties: respective management offices [ ] step 3: request the endorsement stamp – request an osa endorsement stamp via the nusync form (this must be completed by your osa liaison person). [ ] step 4: get stamped – once osa arranges a date and time, head to the osa office to physically stamp your printed posters. [ ] step 5: display & clean up – hang your posters on the designated general noticeboards. you must take down all posters within 3 days after your event ends. for inquiries, email osastudentleadership@nus.edu.sg and review the campus responsible poster usage guidelines. on-campus outdoor banners banner spaces are managed entirely by their respective venue owners. write directly to the specific venue owners to inquire about availability, and make sure to include all of your organization's contact details in the email. (image taken from king edward vii hall's hallplay 2026's banner @ utown stephen raidy centre) reach us info.nuscac@gmail.com where we are nus students' cultural activities club yusof ishak house 31 lower kent ridge road nus cultural activities club about discover nus arts on campus © 2026 national university of singapore, last updated 16 july 2026

title: Equipment Loaning | NUS Cultural Activities Club (CAC)
content: this website is under development. check in again on 1 august 2026. nus cac home venue funding publicity resources student groups equipment loaning about nus cac home venue funding publicity resources student groups equipment loaning about equipment loaning find the tools to pursue your dreams. start now equipment loaning: nus amplified – can provide instruments and a variety of sound equipment such as mixers, di boxes and cables, etc. for more information, please refer to the excel attached below. (file is included in this folder.) to loan equipment, please contact nus amplified at nusamplified@gmail.com nus arts production crew – can provide instruments, sound equipment and lighting equipment. beyond the loaning of equipment, nus arts production crew also provides crewing services. for more information, please refer to this link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1aa52u_bvglrz64a25pplbsm-2zw-l8irultpvgddcg4/edit?gid=0#gid=0 (will shorten the link) listed is the terms and conditions for equipment and/or crew rental: terms of loan the loan period will commence and terminate on the dates agreed upon. any and all extensions of the loan period are subject to written agreement between nus arts production crew and the lending group. the lending group will ensure that the equipment received on the commencement date of the loan period is in satisfactory working equipment. the lending group is to return all equipment to nus arts production crew on the termination date in the same condition it was loaned out. the lending group is liable for any and all damages to the equipment during the loan period. in the event of loss, theft, or damage of any equipment, the lending group will immediately inform nus arts production crew. the lending group will be liable for the full cost of repair of any damaged equipment. the lending group will replace any equipment that is lost, stolen, or damaged beyond repair with an item that is identical or equivalent in value and kind. a fine of $2 per day per piece of equipment will be imposed for late returns. full payment to nus arts production crew will be made one week after the termination date. to loan equipment, please contact nus arts production crew at nusartsproductioncrew@gmail.com reach us info.nuscac@gmail.com where we are nus students' cultural activities club yusof ishak house 31 lower kent ridge road nus cultural activities club about discover nus arts on campus © 2026 national university of singapore, last updated 16 july 2026
